title: callista longicollis
intro: Callista longicollis, also known as the shellflower, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive shell-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Callista longicollis include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
